name: rules-from-coding-agent-failures
description: Capture agent failures, triage logs into closure work, promote patterns into durable fixes, verify closeout, and score Level 4-5 maturity. Triggers: 'reflection log', 'promote failures into rules', 'are we at Level 4/5'. Not for first-pass agent-readiness scaffolding (use harden-repo-for-coding-agents).

inspired_by.name: Engineering Agents — Harness Assessment
inspired_by.author: Engineering Agents
inspired_by.contribution: Level 1–5 maturity rubric; reflection-log artifact as a Level 3 signal; 'weakest discipline is your ceiling.' This skill owns the Level 4–5 extension (Specification Architecture / Sovereign Engineering); `harden-repo-for-coding-agents` owns Levels 1–3.

inspired_by.name: Evaluating AGENTS.md: Are Repository-Level Context Files Helpful for Coding Agents?
inspired_by.author: Mündler et al. (ETH Zürich / LogicStar.ai)
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical: autogenerated context files drop task success ~3% and inflate cost >20%; developer-written files improve by only ~4%. The empirical basis for W1 — the ≥3-observed-failures floor that gates this skill's `promote` workflow.

inspired_by.name: AGENTS.md
inspired_by.author: Agentic AI Foundation (Linux Foundation)
inspired_by.contribution: The instruction-surface artifact that this skill's `promote` workflow writes rules into. AGENTS.md scaffolding itself is handled by `harden-repo-for-coding-agents` (this skill assumes one already exists).

inspired_by.name: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: Token-budget framing: find smallest set of high-signal tokens that maximize the likelihood of desired outcome. Reflection-log entries are on-demand context (read only during `promote`), not always-loaded, so they don't inflate the always-loaded surface.

inspired_by.name: AI Risk Management Framework (and Generative AI Profile)
inspired_by.author: NIST
inspired_by.contribution: Governance, content provenance, pre-deployment testing, incident disclosure. The reflection log is the audit trail for 'human rejected agent recommendation' events that the GenAI profile requires.

metadata.catalog_summary: Capture observed agent failures in a per-file reflection log, triage accumulated entries into failure-mode clusters, promote recurring patterns into durable fixes, and verify closeout. For teams with a feedback signal — eval suites, run-level telemetry, A/B baselines, or a skill catalog under test.
